steps.title: Find the two main claims
steps.content: The passage presents community science as useful in two ways: it helps the public understand scientists' daily work, and it lets researchers collect much more data. The butterfly study is an example of the data benefit, not the whole main idea.

steps.title: Remove partial or false choices
steps.content: Choice A overfocuses on Whitcomb and invents surprise. Choice C contradicts the claim that community science is very effective for gathering data. Choice D keeps the data benefit but denies the public-understanding benefit. The answer is B.
